zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Toly於1325年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾維蒙達哈姆時，她的父親Sir John de Toly24歲，母親Margaret de Tregoz22歲。她與Thomas de Wasteneys結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1392年12月28日在英國林可爾史爾享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Toly于1325年出生在英国萊希斯特斯爾維蒙達哈姆时，她的父亲Sir John de Toly24岁，母亲Margaret de Tregoz22岁。她与Thomas de Wasteneys结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1392年12月28日在英国林可爾史爾享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰林可爾史爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toly
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Czech and Slovak (Holý): from holý ‘naked, bare, bald’, thus a nickname denoting either a young, beardless man, a bald or clean-shaven man, or a destitute person.
English: variant of   Holley  .

variants.name: Holley
meanings: English:
nickname from Middle English hol ‘hollow’ + eie, egh(e) ‘eye’ (Old English hol + ēage), perhaps a nickname for someone with a missing eye.
topographic name for someone who lived by a ‘clearing in a hollow’, from Middle English hol ‘hollow’ + lei(e) ‘woodland clearing’.

variants.name: Hula
meanings: Czech (Hůla): nickname from a variant of the adjective holý ‘bald, bare, or clean-shaven’ (see Holy ).
Rusyn (from Poland and Slovakia; in Slovakia spelled Huľa) and Ukrainian: descriptive nickname for someone with a prominent bump or wart, Ukrainian dialect hulya. Compare Gula 1.
Slovak: cognate of 1 or 2.

variants.name: Holecek
meanings: Czech (Holeček): from a diminutive of holý ‘naked, bare, clean-shaven’ (see Holy ).

variants.name: Hollas
meanings: Czech: from a derivative of holý ‘bald, bare, or clean-shaven’; see Holy .
English (Yorkshire): variant of   Hallows  .

variants.name: Holan
meanings: Czech: nickname from a derivative of holý ‘naked, bare, or clean-shaven’ (see Holy ).
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farms, chiefly in Trøndelag, named with the definite plural form of Old Norse hóll ‘round hill, mound’.

variants.name: Holec
meanings: Czech: nickname for a beardless young man, from a noun derivative of holý ‘naked, bare’ (see Holy ).

variants.name: Holik
meanings: Czech and Slovak (Holík): nickname derived from holý ‘naked, bare, bald’ (see Holy ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Toly was born in 1325, in Wymondham, Leicestershire, England, her father, Sir John de Toly, was 24 and her mother, Margaret de Tregoz, was 22. She married Thomas de Wasteneys. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 28 December 1392, in Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 67, and was buried in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Toly est née en 1325, à Wymondham, Leicestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Toly, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Tregoz, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas de Wasteneys. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 décembre 1392, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterrée à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Toly 1325 in Wymondham, Leicestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Toly 24 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Tregoz 22. Sie hat Thomas de Wasteneys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. Dezember 1392 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Lincolnshire, England und wurde in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Toly è nata il nel 1325 nel Wymondham, Leicestershire, England, sua padre, Sir John de Toly, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Tregoz, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Thomas de Wasteneys. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 28 dicembre 1392, nel Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolta a Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Tolyが1325年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアウイモンドハムで生まれた時，父Sir John de Tolyは24歳，母Margaret de Tregozは22歳であった。Thomas de Wasteneysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1392年12月28日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Toly(이)가 1325년 영국 레이스스터실르 윈이몽함에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Toly(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Tregoz(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 Thomas de Wasteneys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1392년 12월 28일 영국 링코실르에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Toly nasceu em 1325, em Wymondham, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Toly, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Tregoz, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas de Wasteneys. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de dezembro de 1392, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Toly родилась в 1325 г. в местности вимондхам, лисестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Toly было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Tregoz, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas de Wasteneys. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 28 декабря 1392 г. в местности линколншир, Англия в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Toly nació en 1325, en Wymondham, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Toly, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret de Tregoz, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Thomas de Wasteneys. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 28 de diciembre de 1392, en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultada en Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
